verdict_reasoning: LYN is a non-receptor Src-family tyrosine kinase with an N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation sites that mediate stable association with the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane. While primarily cytoplasmic, LYN is tethered to the PM via dual lipidation and participates in signal transduction from surface receptors (BCR, FcγR, etc.). The lipid anchors keep it stably membrane-associated and accessible to phosphorylate PM-resident receptors; this constitutes a stable membrane association via its own mechanism (post-translational lipidation). LYN is a canonical example of inner-leaflet signaling kinase with functional PM residence.
